comment.body:
Thanks for your work in doing the review, Alma! I read your data that the strongest part of your model is the risk levels so guess I'm just buying calls at upper risk and puts at lower risk level once broken through, not playing on high volatility days:) With the occasional bottom reversion play like today. 

Statistically speaking, this should bring your models edge up even further than that 60% range, right? :)

comment.body:
But this should help with accuracy rate then, shouldn't it?
If it's effectively restricting the trades to the highest probability plays of your model. Your model sets the pieces for the play, and the accuracy is spread out across the categories, with risk level breakthrough as the highest accuracy within your model. Right?
So over the long run, by focusing on only this highest accuracy set play, accuracy should be better than by playing all your models suggested plays (although these are obviously based on the trader and the trades one makes etc.). But in terms of the plays, much like swing traders when they check their trading statistics for one pattern (let's say cup and handle breakout of previous ATH Vs. Consolidating trianlg pattern) one comes out superior, over the long term their performance is better by focusing on the higher accuracy plays. Or does that not apply here to your model?
